fields.label: Kerberos server name
fields.description: The Kerberos service name to use (optional). Default is 'postgres'.

fields.label: TLS/SSL Method
fields.description: This option determines how TLS/SSL certifications are configured. Selecting File system path will allow you to configure certificates by specifying paths to existing certificates on the local file system where Grafana is running. Be sure that the file is readable by the user executing the Grafana process. Selecting Certificate content will allow you to configure certificates by specifying its content. The content will be stored encrypted in Grafana's database. When connecting to the database the certificates will be written as files to Grafana's configured data path on the local file system.

fields.label: TLS/SSL Root Certificate
fields.description: If the selected TLS/SSL mode requires a server root certificate, provide the path to the file here.

fields.label: TLS/SSL Client Certificate
fields.description: To authenticate with an TLS/SSL client certificate, provide the path to the file here. Be sure that the file is readable by the user executing the grafana process.

fields.label: TLS/SSL Client Key
fields.description: To authenticate with a client TLS/SSL certificate, provide the path to the corresponding key file here. Be sure that the file is only readable by the user executing the grafana process.

fields.label: TLS/SSL Root Certificate
fields.description: If the selected TLS/SSL mode requires a server root certificate, provide it here.

fields.label: TLS/SSL Client Certificate
fields.description: To authenticate with an TLS/SSL client certificate, provide the client certificate here.

fields.label: TLS/SSL Client Key
fields.description: To authenticate with a client TLS/SSL certificate, provide the key here.

fields.label: Max open
fields.description: The maximum number of open connections to the database. If Max idle connections is greater than 0 and the Max open connections is less than Max idle connections, then Max idle connections will be reduced to match the Max open connections limit. If set to 0, there is no limit on the number of open connections.

fields.label: Auto max idle
fields.description: If enabled, automatically set the number of Maximum idle connections to the same value as Max open connections. If the number of maximum open connections is not set it will be set to the default.

fields.label: Max idle
fields.description: The maximum number of connections in the idle connection pool.If Max open connections is greater than 0 but less than the Max idle connections, then the Max idle connections will be reduced to match the Max open connections limit. If set to 0, no idle connections are retained.

fields.label: Max lifetime
fields.description: The maximum amount of time in seconds a connection may be reused. If set to 0, connections are reused forever.

fields.label: Query timeout
fields.description: The maximum amount of time in seconds to wait for a query to complete. Valid range: 5-600 seconds (10 minutes). If set to 0, the default of 30 seconds will be used.

fields.label: krb5 config file path
fields.description: The path to the configuration file for the [MIT krb5 package](https://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-1.12/doc/admin/conf_files/krb5_conf.html). The default is `/etc/krb5.conf`.

fields.label: TLS/SSL Mode
fields.description: This option determines whether or with what priority a secure TLS/SSL TCP/IP connection will be negotiated with the server

relationships.description: File-path TLS credentials — used when authType is 'TLS/SSL Authentication', sslmode is not 'disable', and tlsConfigurationMethod is 'file-path'. All three paths are validated together by the backend (IsValidFilePathTLS, pkg/plugin/tlsmanager.go:25-36).

Inline TLS credentials — used when authType is 'TLS/SSL Authentication', sslmode is not 'disable', and tlsConfigurationMethod is 'file-content'. All three secrets are validated together (IsValidFileContentTLS, pkg/plugin/tlsmanager.go:39-50) and written to Grafana's data path as files before connecting.

Kerberos credentials — used when authType is 'Kerberos Authentication'. credentialCache and configFilePath feed the krb5 connection string; kerberosServerName sets the optional krbsrvname (pkg/plugin/settings.go:130-141).

instructions.msg: The CockroachDB datasource has three authentication methods, selected via jsonData.authType: 'SQL Authentication', 'Kerberos Authentication', and 'TLS/SSL Authentication'. Unlike most datasources, ALL connection fields live in jsonData — url, database and user are read from jsonData (pkg/plugin/settings.go:28-41,247), NOT from the root-level datasource settings. Only the password (and TLS certificate content) live in secureJsonData. Configure exactly one auth method; changing authType in the editor clears user, credentialCache and password.

SQL Authentication (username/password over the PostgreSQL wire protocol). Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authType":"SQL Authentication","url":"localhost:26257","database":"defaultdb","user":"<user>"},"secureJsonData":{"password":"<password>"}}. url, database and user are always required; password is required for every auth method EXCEPT Kerberos (pkg/plugin/settings.go:54-67).

TLS/SSL Authentication. Set jsonData.sslmode ('disable'|'require'|'verify-ca'|'verify-full', editor default 'require') and jsonData.tlsConfigurationMethod (editor default 'file-content', which differs from grafana-postgresql-datasource's 'file-path'). When sslmode != 'disable': 'file-path' reads jsonData.sslRootCertFile / sslCertFile / sslKeyFile as paths on the Grafana host (all three required); 'file-content' reads secureJsonData.tlsCACert / tlsClientCert / tlsClientKey as inline PEM (all three required) which the backend writes to Grafana's data path before connecting. A password is still required. Example: {"jsonData":{"authType":"TLS/SSL Authentication","url":"host:26257","database":"defaultdb","user":"<user>","sslmode":"verify-full","tlsConfigurationMethod":"file-content"},"secureJsonData":{"password":"<pw>","tlsCACert":"<PEM>","tlsClientCert":"<PEM>","tlsClientKey":"<PEM>"}}.

Kerberos Authentication needs NO password. Provide jsonData.credentialCache (required, e.g. '/tmp/krb5cc_1000'), jsonData.configFilePath (krb5 config path, editor default '/etc/krb5.conf') and optionally jsonData.kerberosServerName (krbsrvname, default 'postgres'). The backend always negotiates sslmode=require with authenticator=krb5 (pkg/plugin/settings.go:130-141). Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authType":"Kerberos Authentication","url":"host:26257","database":"defaultdb","user":"<user>","credentialCache":"/tmp/krb5cc_1000","configFilePath":"/etc/krb5.conf"}}.

secureJsonData values (password, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.password == true) instead of secureJsonData.

Connection/URL: jsonData.url must be 'host:port' (e.g. 'localhost:26257' — CockroachDB's default port is 26257, not the PostgreSQL 5432); do not add a scheme. Connection-pool knobs are jsonData.maxOpenConns (default 5), maxIdleConns (default 2), maxIdleConnsAuto (frontend-only; auto-syncs maxIdleConns to maxOpenConns), connMaxLifetime (seconds, default 300) and queryTimeout (seconds, default 30, clamped to 5-600). Pitfall: the backend replaces a stored 0 with the default (5/2/300/30) at load time (pkg/plugin/settings.go:254-265), so 0 does NOT mean 'unlimited'/'reuse forever' despite the tooltips.
